title: artemisia laciniata subsp. parryi
intro: Artemisia laciniata subsp. parryi, also known as Parry's sagebrush, is a subspecies of the artemisia laciniata plant, a type of shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic qualities. The health benefits associated with Parry's sagebrush include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues such as colds and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through various methods, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to address a range of health concerns.
